title: pelma bacilliferum
side_effects: Pelma bacilliferum can have side effects if used improperly, such as nausea, dizziness, and headaches due to excessive consumption of its toxic alkaloids. Prolonged exposure to the plant's volatile compounds can cause skin irritation, allergic reactions, and respiratory problems in some individuals. In severe cases, improper use of pelma bacilliferum can lead to hallucinations, tremors, and even cardiac issues. It is essential to handle and consume the plant under the guidance of a medical professional or a qualified herbalist to avoid these adverse effects.
